coreConcept: The water cycle is the continuous movement of water between Earth and the sky. Water changes form—liquid, vapor, and sometimes ice—and travels through evaporation, condensation, and precipitation. This cycle recycles water so plants, animals, and people can use it.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: The Sun
keyActorsAndComponents.description: Heats water so it can evaporate and start the cycle.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: Oceans, Lakes, and Rivers
keyActorsAndComponents.description: Hold most of Earth’s water and provide liquid water to evaporate.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: The Atmosphere
keyActorsAndComponents.description: Holds water vapor, helps clouds form, and moves moisture around the planet.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: Plants (Transpiration)
keyActorsAndComponents.description: Give off water from leaves which adds moisture to the air.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: Land and Soil
keyActorsAndComponents.description: Collects rain, lets water soak in (infiltration), or sends it along rivers (runoff).

storyOfProcess.name: Evaporation and Transpiration
storyOfProcess.description: Sun heats water in oceans, lakes, and soils; water becomes vapor and plants release vapor too.

storyOfProcess.name: Condensation
storyOfProcess.description: Water vapor cools in the air and forms tiny droplets that make clouds.

storyOfProcess.name: Precipitation
storyOfProcess.description: Droplets or ice fall from clouds as rain, snow, sleet, or hail back to Earth.

storyOfProcess.name: Collection and Infiltration
storyOfProcess.description: Water collects in bodies of water, soaks into the ground to feed plants and aquifers, or flows as runoff to rivers and oceans.

corePrinciples.name: Conservation of Water
corePrinciples.description: Water is recycled and its total amount stays roughly the same on Earth.

corePrinciples.name: Energy Drives Change
corePrinciples.description: Solar energy powers evaporation and weather that move water around.

corePrinciples.name: Phase and State Changes
corePrinciples.description: Water changes between liquid, vapor, and solid, which lets it move through different parts of the cycle.
